message.content:
I need to understand a mobile layout bug in PostCards.jsx. The `.reading-time-text` and `.user-post-settings` elements are not showing properly on mobile — they appear at the bottom instead of being visible in the card.

Please explore thoroughly:

1. Read `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` — focus on:
   - How `.reading-time-container` / `.reading-time-text` is rendered
   - How `.user-post-settings` is rendered
   - The overall card structure (what's inside `.cards`, `.card-content`, `.card-icons-container`, `.user-info`)

2. Read `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/postcards.css` — focus on:
   - All mobile styles (`@media (max-width: 480px)` section)
   - The `.card-icons-container` layout (desktop + mobile)
   - `.reading-time-container` / `.reading-time-text` styles
   - `.user-post-settings` styles
   - `.user-info` and `.user-info-child-container` styles
   - How flex wrapping works in the footer area

3. Trace the exact DOM hierarchy of a single card to understand why these elements end up at the bottom on mobile.

Provide the full JSX structure of a card (the return JSX from PostCards showing how user-info, card-content, card-icons-container are nested) and all relevant CSS for both desktop and mobile.

content.text: I'll help you understand the mobile layout bug in PostCards. Let me start by exploring the component structure and CSS.
